experiment: Chavez Transform sigma-variation — commutator surrogate at varying offsets from critical line
description: 16D commutator surrogate vectors constructed as 2(sigma-1/2)*[u_antisym, F_base] pattern at varying sigma offsets. Transform value at sigma=1/2 should be exactly 0 if commutator formula holds.
interpretation: Transform vanishes exactly at sigma=1/2 and grows monotonically off it. Relationship approximately linear in sigma-offset. CAILculator independently reproduces the commutator vanishing structure of the algebraic formula 2*(sigma-1/2)*[u_antisym, F_base t] without being given the formula.
phase_70_relevance: Empirically validates bilateral_collapse_continuation — the scalar (s.re - 1/2) is the exact quantity that forces the transform to zero at the critical line.
